method: LLM selector (from union of A+B) + Quant allocator (MIQP-equivalent, LAMBDA=2.0)

per_pick_rationale.rationale: Deeply discounted infrastructure hybrid with an accelerating Data segment, defensive inflation-linked cash flows, and a ~5% yield floor; the market has over-discounted structural complexity.

Commercial-stage biotech with a validated IBSRELA revenue inflection and de-risked balance sheet; offers positive expected value despite uncertainty around XPHOZAH economics and working-capital conversion.
Dominant ANZ employment marketplace with widening placement share and margin recovery; the severe narrative-driven selloff creates a convex recovery opportunity with manageable leverage.
Imminent Coterra merger close and pre-committed dividend/buyback step-up provide near-term catalyst visibility in a financially fortified E&P name trading at a discount to peers.
Global entertainment utility with software-like 32% operating margins, a $25bn buyback floor, and a valuation reset that no longer requires multiple expansion to deliver attractive base-case returns.
World’s largest custom mining truck body fabricator trading at distressed levels; August 2026 earnings represent a high-beta bifurcation point if the multi-region turnaround stabilizes.
Dominant CPAP leader with best-in-class margins, net-cash balance sheet, and 12% mask resupply growth; GLP-1 demand-destruction fears appear overdone and Philips’ absence sustains pricing power.
Core advertising engine gaining share with 40% operating margins and a fortress balance sheet; AI capex fears have compressed the forward multiple to ~16.6x despite robust earnings power.

Tirzepatide volume flywheel continues to overwhelm pricing headwinds in metabolic disease; manufacturing scale, Medicare expansion, and retatrutide optionality support a premium but durable growth outlook.
Indispensable global payment network with durable cross-border recovery and aggressive buybacks; temporary macro fears have created a modest discount to a high-ROIC compounder.
Mission-critical enterprise workflow platform with 97% renewal rates and a $27.7bn backlog; AI displacement fears look overstated relative to observable operational resilience.
Irreplaceable nuclear fleet backed by long-duration tech PPAs provides near-term earnings visibility and scarcity value; regulatory overhang is bounded by a fully hedged 2026 book.

Hybrid AI silicon and VMware software utility with best-in-class cash conversion; a $73bn backlog offers near-term certainty, though customer concentration and EU regulatory risk warrant allocator awareness.
Dominant high-speed Ethernet fabric architect with a pristine balance sheet and hyperscaler backlog; supply-constrained growth is durable but the premium valuation leaves little room for error.
AI infrastructure backbone with unmatched supply-chain lock-up and a derated forward multiple; near-term revenue visibility is mechanically strong even as long-term demand durability remains debated.

llm_parsed.comparison_notes: Track A was a pure quant MILP optimization that maximized probability-weighted expected return subject to a quadratic concentration penalty, producing weights heavily skewed toward the highest raw expected-return names—BIP, ARDX, and SEK.AX received double-digit allocations—while min-capping ANET, NVDA, and AVGO at 3%. Track B was a pure LLM construction that qualitatively assessed conviction, macro factor exposures, and pairwise correlations, resulting in more evenly distributed weights and deliberate underweights to names with negatively skewed risk/reward (ANET, NVDA, AVGO) despite their high expected returns. Because the two tracks selected the exact same 15-name universe and every name satisfies the hard constraints, the optimal final selection is the full union. We delegate sizing to the quant allocator, preserving the consensus security list while allowing the optimization engine to manage concentration, correlation, and sector limits.
llm_parsed.portfolio_thesis: The consolidated portfolio blends durable compounders (NFLX, MA, META, LLY), event-driven recoveries (SEK.AX, DVN, ANG.AX, BIP), and defensive cash generators (RMD, VST) across infrastructure, healthcare, technology, energy, and financial services. By retaining all 15 names, we capture the full spectrum of probability-weighted expected returns and idiosyncratic catalysts identified by both quantitative and qualitative processes. The quant allocator will size positions to respect sector caps and correlation penalties, producing a globally diversified long-only book with a defensive floor from regulated utilities, medical devices, and payment networks, and upside optionality from AI infrastructure, metabolic drug growth, and cyclical re-ratings.
key_risks: Correlated AI capex cycle exposure across AVGO, NVDA, ANET, and META could simultaneously underperform if hyperscaler demand moderates or custom-silicon displacement accelerates.
Cyclical and commodity-linked names (DVN, ANG.AX, SEK.AX) remain vulnerable to macro deceleration, oil price normalization, or softening labor markets.
Healthcare reimbursement and regulatory shifts could impair ARDX and LLY if pricing or policy headwinds intensify beyond base-case assumptions.
Leverage and refinancing risks in BIP and VST may widen if credit spreads deteriorate or infrastructure regulatory rulings prove adverse.
